title: The Final Exit

"'''The Final Exit'''" is a short story written by [[Patricia A. Jackson]] that was originally published in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 4|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 4]] by [[West End Games]] in November [[1994]]. It was reprinted in ''[[The Best of the Star Wars Adventure Journal, Issues 1-4]]'' as well as the short-story anthology ''[[Tales from the Empire]]''. It was later archived online at [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] on [[October 1]], [[2004]]. It was also included in a Czech-language anthology, ''[[Setkání v mlze]]'', in [[2009]].

==Plot summary==
A moody [[Dark Jedi/Legends|fallen Jedi]] named [[Adalric Cessius Brandl|Aldaric Brandl]] hires a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] to take him to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Trulalis]]. The [[Dark side adept/Legends|dark sider]] pays for the ship’s [[Captain/Legends|captian]], [[Thaddeus Ross]], to accompany him to [[Kovit|Kovit settlement]]. There, Adalric visits the [[Kovit theater|theatre stage]] where he once performed as an [[Actor/Legends|actor]], seeking forgiveness from his old mentor, master [[Otias Atori|Otias]]. But there is no salvation to be found in the past for Adalric – not in his former career onstage, nor in the presence of his [[Youngling/Legends|young]] son, [[Jaalib Brandl|Jaalib]].

Leaving Trulalis aboard Thaddeus’ vessel, Adalric is tracked down by the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Imperial Star Destroyer]] [[Interrogator/Legends|''Interrogator'']], [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of [[High Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne|Tremayne]]. The Imperials capture Adalric, and Thaddeus is surprised to receive a hefty portion of a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] for returning a fugitive to the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]]. Adalric, however, does not surrender himself quietly. Stealing one of Thaddeus’ [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]], he creates havoc aboard the Star Destroyer.

==Continuity==
Though the story left [[Adalric Cessius Brandl|Adalric’s]] fate vague – he might have [[Death/Legends|perished]] in the [[Explosive charge/Legends|explosion]] – he proved to be alive in the follow up tale, [[Uhl Eharl Khoehng (short story)|''Uhl Eharl Khoehng'']], set in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="ERC" />
